Off Calendar May-13-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. BEN NISENBAUM, Esq. of Burris, Nisenbaum, Curry and Lacy, LLP (tel: 510-839-5200) for Plaintiffs.
Atty. AMY SHEARER, Esq. of San Francisco Police Department (tel: 415-553-1192) for San Francisco Police Department.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFFS TERRY BROWN AS CO-SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST TO DECEDENT CHYNNA BROWN (A.K.A. BANKO BROWN), AND KEVINISHA HENDERSON's Motion For An In-Camera Hearing To Determine The Privileged San Francisco Police Department Investigative Records That Must Be Released To Plaintiffs Pursuant To The Courts Protective Order.

Matter discussed. Counsel Amy Shearer provides to the Court Sgt. McWilliams's chronological report. The Court orders that document be released to Plaintiffs as well. The Court provides clarification as to the order. San Francisco Police Department has requested to redact some information in the investigation prior to releasing the documents to Plaintiffs. The Court states Plaintiffs are entitled to the contact information for the witnesses. The San Francisco Police Department shall not redact residential addresses, email addresses and phone numbers for witnesses. The San Francisco Police Department is to redact birth dates, driver's licenses, social security numbers and criminal history. Moreover, pursuant to code of civil procedure 129, the San Francisco Police Department is to redact photographs depicting the decedant. The tentative ruling is adopted.

Plaintiffs Terry Brown et al. seek production of San Francisco Police Department records related to its investigation of the April 27, 2023 shooting that gives rise to this case. Plaintiffs and SFPD have stipulated that the court will review in camera those records to determine whether any should be released to Plaintiffs, applying the factors set out in Evidence Code section 1040 and applicable caselaw. Importantly, Plaintiffs have agreed to treat any disclosed information confidentially pursuant to a protective order entered in this court on December 9, 2024. (See Order of Dec. 9, 2024; Stipulation and Order, Apr. 22, 2025, p. 3:17-21.)

Evidence Code section 1040(b)(2) requires the court to balance the need for confidentiality of official information against the public interest in disclosure. Here, the court orders disclosure only to Plaintiffs in confidence pursuant to the December 9 Order; any broader disclosure would require a further court order. The court finds that Plaintiffs' interest in the information is significant for the reasons stated in the parties' stipulation at 2:24-27. That significant need must be balanced against the official interests in secrecy. Here, that includes that the District Attorney could pursue charges related to the shooting in the future and that there is a general public interest in permitting investigating agencies to develop information in confidence. Those are important interests, but the court finds them somewhat attenuated for present purposes, because there is no indication that the investigating agency is actively involved in this case at present, and because the December 9 Order makes the disclosure here a limited one.

Accordingly the court orders SFPD to disclose those items specified in the In Camera Review worksheets, a copy of which will be provided to SFPD and Plaintiffs' counsel at the May 13 hearing. The court appreciates the parties' cooperative efforts in narrowing the issues in dispute and presenting them cleanly to the court for resolution.

Off Calendar May-22-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. BEN NISENBAUM, Esq. and Atty. BRANDON YEE, Esq. of Burris, Nisenbaum, Curry & Lacy LLP (tel: 510-839-5200) for Plaintiffs.
Atty. JIGAR DESAI, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Birgaard & Smith LLP (tel: 925-357-3456) for Defendants.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT WALGREENS BOOTS ALLIANCE, INC AND WALGREENS CO.'s DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Walgreens Co. and Walgreens Boots Alliance, Inc.'s demurrer to the first amended complaint's (FAC) premises liability count 6 is overruled. The FAC adequately pleads the elements of premises liability. (FAC 18:11-19:21; CACI 1000.) Defendants say "a physical defect of the property" must be pled (Memo. 6:18-19), but they cite no authority so holding and CACI 1000(2) is to the contrary: defendant need only be "negligent in the use or maintenance of the property." CACI 1003 applies only to the subset of cases involving "unsafe conditions," not all premises liability cases. (See id. at use note.) Defendants also say the premises liability count is "duplicative" of others (Memo. 7:10), but a complaint "may plead in the alternative." (Adams v. Paul (1995) 11 Cal.4th 583, 593.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
